records.ety: [Akin to OS. drōm, D. droom, G. traum, Icel. draumr, Dan. & Sw. dröm; cf. G. trügen to deceive, Skr. druh to harm, hurt, try to hurt. AS. drem joy, gladness, and OS. drōm joy are, perh., different words; cf. Gr. qry^los noise.]

defs: The thoughts, or series of thoughts, or imaginary transactions, which occupy the mind during sleep; a sleeping vision.
quotes: Dreams are but interludes which fancy makes.
I had a dream which was not all a dream.

defs: A visionary scheme; a wild conceit; an idle fancy; a vagary; a revery; -- in this sense, applied to an imaginary or anticipated state of happiness; as, a dream of bliss; the dream of his youth.
quotes: There sober thought pursued the amusing theme, Till Fancy colored it and formed a dream.
It is not them a mere dream, but a very real aim which they propose.

records.ety: [Cf. AS. drēman, drman, to rejoice. See Dream, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dreamed (drēmd) or Dreamt (drmt); p. pr. & vb. n. Dreaming.]

defs: To have ideas or images in the mind while in the state of sleep; to experience sleeping visions; -- often with of; as, to dream of a battle, or of an absent friend.
To let the mind run on in idle revery or vagary; to anticipate vaguely as a coming and happy reality; to have a visionary notion or idea; to imagine.
quotes: Here may we sit and dream Over the heavenly theme
They dream on in a constant course of reading, but not digesting

defs: To have a dream of; to see, or have a vision of, in sleep, or in idle fancy; -- often followed by an objective clause.
quotes: Your old men shall dream dreams
At length in sleep their bodies they compose, And dreamt the future fight
And still they dream that they shall still succeed
